title: "What Shall We Eat The Seventh Year?" Leviticus 25:1-7, 18-22

notes.introduction: An exposition of Leviticus 25:1-7 and 18-22 on the Sabbath year law given to Israel, tracing the twin themes of faith and obedience through God's command to rest the land every seventh year and his promise of a threefold harvest in the sixth year.

sections.heading: Faith and Obedience as Biblical Themes
The Plan of God: The Sabbath Year
The Manna Parallel: Obedience and Supply
The Promise of God: Threefold Harvest
Manna Ceasing at Entry into Canaan
Israel's Disobedience and the Babylonian Captivity
Principles of Faith and Obedience Applied
